podcasts.name: Davie Fogarty
podcasts.title: Making money pushed me away from people
podcasts.transcript: What problem are you trying to solve from moving out here? >> I think when I bought my property in the Adelaide Hills, it was at like [music] a very bad time for me. I just made a ton of money, but I was just getting super super anxious. And it was all just like social anxiety being around people. So, the logical explanation is like get away from people. I obviously love nature, I love the animals, I love spending time up there, but at the same time that is the wrong approach to that stage of your life. It's a very natural thing when you start to make money to start to seclude yourself. You buy your neighborhoods that your family can't afford, your friends can't afford. You start going doing different things that are probably contradictory to the true things that you value. And I think that's what moving into the middle of nowhere did. And one of the things that I I value most is entrepreneurship. Now, I love Adelaide. I want to help the entrepreneurship within Adelaide heaps, but you know, just like next door I've got Toby Pearce, one of my good friends. Sold his company for, I don't know, 200 plus million. Then I've got Simon Beard just down the road there as well. He sold his company for like 600 million as well. So, the entrepreneurship within Gold Coast is huge. We've got so many daily mental clients here. So, yeah, I'm just trying to throw myself back into the deep end, surround myself with really good people, absorb all of that energy. But more importantly, I'm just trying to get away from the freezing cold winters.
